notes: EV_FMAND ([...] forced-eval turning a failed function-name match into a hard '#-1 FUNCTION (name) NOT FOUND' error instead of a literal fallback) localizes to the smallest enclosing argument/catenated-argument boundary, not the whole containing [...] block: [cat(add(1,2),badname(3))] keeps add(1,2)'s '3 ' and cat()'s join with the second argument despite badname(3) failing, and [lcstr(HELLO,add(1,2))extra] keeps the outer 'extra' -- lcstr() legitimately receives the (uppercase) error text as its own argument and lowercases it, which is why that one case's message looks lowercase (no special-casing of the message itself is needed). A bare/top-level failure (nothing else in that [...] block's own scan, e.g. [badname(1)]) or a failure that's the sole content of a nested [...] block (e.g. [stuff1 [%q0(1,2)] stuff2]) still collapses to just the error for that whole block, since there's no sibling content at that scan level to preserve.
